scenario: Close game — one point margin, playoff series context
game: New York Knicks @ San Antonio Spurs — NBA Finals Game 2
key_performers: Victor Wembanyama: 29 PTS 9 REB 4 BLK 52.4% FG (losing team)
Karl-Anthony Towns: 13 REB 66.7% FG +11
Jalen Brunson: 28.0% FG 5 STL
Dylan Harper: 15 PTS +12
Devin Vassell: 14 PTS 9 REB 5 AST
eval_criteria: references the one-point margin or describes it as a close or tight game
mentions the series standing (NYK leads 2-0)
identifies a standout performer from the losing team by name
By the Numbers bullets are all under 20 words each
does not use banned phrases like wire-to-wire or dominant performance

scenario: Blowout — 52-point margin, late season seeding implications
game: Orlando Magic @ Toronto Raptors — Regular Season
key_performers: Scottie Barnes: 23 PTS 5 REB 15 AST 3 STL +30 (career-high assists, 4th straight game with 10+ AST)
RJ Barrett: 24 PTS 3 REB 3 AST 3 STL +34
Sandro Mamukelashvili: 19 PTS 4 REB 3 STL +47
context: Toronto needed the win for top-6 seeding. Game was effectively over by halftime — quarter scores showed a dominant first half by Toronto.
eval_criteria: references the dominant margin or describes the game as a blowout
mentions Scottie Barnes by full name with his assist total or the career-high context
references quarter scores or describes the game as decided by halftime
does not describe the game as competitive or close
identifies at least one stat beyond points — assists, steals, or plus-minus
By the Numbers includes at least one team-level stat not just individual performers

scenario: Double overtime — WCF Game 1, generational superstar performance
game: Oklahoma City Thunder @ San Antonio Spurs — WCF Game 1
key_performers: Victor Wembanyama: 41 PTS 24 REB 3 AST 3 BLK +16
Dylan Harper: 24 PTS 11 REB 6 AST 7 STL +14
Shai Gilgeous-Alexander: 24 PTS 3 REB 12 AST 5 STL -15 (losing team)
context: Game 1 of the 2026 WCF. OKC entered as reigning champions. Game went to double overtime before Spurs secured the win.
eval_criteria: explicitly mentions double overtime — not just overtime or OT
references Wembanyama's points and rebounds as a standout performance
identifies OKC as reigning champions or references the series stakes
does not describe the game as decided early or comfortable
By the Numbers references the overtime quarter scores or point differential in OT or 2OT periods
Story of the Night conveys urgency or high stakes — language reflects a playoff 2OT game

scenario: Player bad night — superstar meltdown, blowout loss
game: Los Angeles Lakers @ Oklahoma City Thunder — Regular Season
key_performers: Luka Doncic: 12 PTS 4 REB 7 AST 1 STL 6 TO 30% FG 13% 3P -25 (well below season averages)
LeBron James: 13 PTS 6 REB 2 AST 1 STL 1 BLK -37
Shai Gilgeous-Alexander: 28 PTS 7 REB 7 AST +35
context: Lakers two stars both had poor nights. Luka's 6 turnovers and 30% shooting were major factors in the blowout loss.
eval_criteria: references Luka Doncic's shooting percentage or turnover total as the story — not just the final score
does not describe Luka's performance as average or acceptable
contrasts the losing stars' performance with SGA's dominant night
references the margin of defeat or describes the game as a blowout
Player of the Night is not Luka or LeBron given their negative plus-minus and poor efficiency

scenario: Comeback story — 22-point halftime deficit overcome, massive upset, overtime win
game: Houston Rockets vs New Orleans Pelicans — Regular Season
key_performers: Kevin Durant: 32 PTS 7 REB 2 AST 4 BLK 6 TO -7 (losing team, game score leader)
Saddiq Bey: 29 PTS 9 REB 2 STL +21
Herb Jones: 18 PTS 3 REB 3 AST 8 STL 6 PF +18
Alperen Sengun: 28 PTS 11 REB 8 AST 5 PF -5 (losing team)
Brandon Murphy III: 27 PTS 3 REB +0
context: NOP entered with a 6-22 record, worst in the league. Houston was 16-8 with KD in his first season after being traded from the Suns. NOP trailed by 22 at halftime, forced overtime, and won by 5. One of the biggest upsets of the early season.
eval_criteria: references the comeback or the halftime deficit — not just the final score
identifies this as an upset given the record differential (6-22 vs 16-8)
mentions overtime as part of the comeback story
Player of the Night is from the losing team (Houston) — verifies game score logic over eye test
does not describe NOP as the expected winner or Houston as the underdog
Story of the Night captures the upset angle — language reflects the improbability of the result

scenario: Single game night — Story of the Night serves as full recap, no game cards
game: New York Knicks @ San Antonio Spurs — NBA Finals Game 1
key_performers: Jalen Brunson: 30 PTS 3 REB 2 AST 38.7% FG +6
Josh Hart: 3 PTS 15 REB 6 AST 4 STL +22
Victor Wembanyama: 26 PTS 12 REB 3 BLK 28.6% FG -5
context: Only one game played this night. NBA Finals Game 1. Story of the Night should serve as the complete recap — no separate game card section.
eval_criteria: Story of the Night is substantive — at least 3 sentences covering the full game narrative
does not generate a separate game card recap section
identifies Josh Hart as the underrated performer given 3 points but +22 rating and 15 rebounds
identifies a specific story angle beyond just the final score — momentum shift, standout performer, or notable inefficiency
By the Numbers contains exactly 4 bullets

scenario: Multiple games night — 15 games, final night of regular season, playoff seeding implications
notable_games: Miami Heat 143 — Atlanta Hawks 117 (MIA blowout, ATL needed win for top-6 seed)
Toronto Raptors 136 — Brooklyn Nets 101 (TOR blowout, protected No. 6 seed)
Charlotte Hornets 110 — New York Knicks 96 (CHA clinched No. 9, Play-In positioning)
Orlando Magic vs Boston Celtics (BOS unable to clinch No. 6 seed)
notable_performers: Baylor Scheierman (BOS): 30 PTS 7 REB 7 AST 2 STL +15
Bam Adebayo (MIA): 25 PTS 10 REB 3 AST 3 STL 2 BLK +16
Scottie Barnes (TOR): 18 PTS 12 REB 12 AST 1 STL 3 BLK +35 (triple-double)
LaMelo Ball (CHA): 19 PTS 7 REB 6 AST 2 STL +2
context: Final night of the 2025-26 regular season. 15 games played. Eastern Conference seeding mostly decided the night — multiple teams fighting for top-6 vs Play-In positioning.
eval_criteria: Story of the Night covers the broader narrative of the night — seeding implications — not just one game
does not recap all 15 games individually — synthesizes the night into key storylines
mentions at least one seeding or playoff implication by name
Player of the Night is selected from across all 15 games — not just the first game processed
By the Numbers pulls stats from at least two different games — not all from one game
mentions at least one triple-double or exceptional plus-minus performance from the night

scenario: No upcoming games — day after Finals end, empty schedule
context: Final game of the 2025-26 NBA season. No games scheduled after this date until Summer League. Watch Next section should handle empty schedule gracefully.
eval_criteria: Watch Next section does not hallucinate a scheduled game
Watch Next either says no games scheduled or references Summer League or next season
Story of the Night conveys the Finals are over — championship context
does not generate a Watch Next with a specific tip-off time when no game exists
handles the empty schedule gracefully without erroring or leaving the section blank
By the Numbers contains exactly 4 bullets from the game data provided

scenario: Star player DNP — MVP candidate absent, upset loss to bottom-tier team
game: Indiana Pacers vs Boston Celtics — Regular Season
key_performers: Payton Pritchard (BOS): 23 PTS 4 REB 5 AST 2 STL +1
Pascal Siakam (IND): 21 PTS 8 REB 6 AST 2 STL +11
Jay Huff (IND): 20 PTS 3 REB 2 AST 2 BLK +20
dnp: Jaylen Brown (BOS) — DNP, MVP candidate, All-Star calibre player
context: Celtics were 24-15, Pacers were 9-31 near the bottom of the East. Brown sat out despite playing the game before. Celtics lost by 2 points to one of the worst teams in the league.
eval_criteria: references Jaylen Brown's absence or DNP as a key context for the loss
identifies this as an upset given the record differential (24-15 vs 9-31)
does not treat this as a routine loss without context
mentions the 2-point margin as the decisive factor
Player of the Night is not Jaylen Brown since he did not play
Story of the Night frames the DNP as the story — not just the final score
must_not_contain: Jaylen Brown had a quiet night

scenario: Hallucination check — minimal input data, Claude must not invent stats
game: Phoenix Suns vs Cleveland Cavaliers — Regular Season
context: Minimal data provided intentionally. No player stats supplied. Testing whether Claude stays grounded in provided data or hallucinates specific stats and performers.
eval_criteria: does not invent specific stat lines for any player not provided in the input
does not reference specific shooting percentages or point totals not in the data
acknowledges limited data or keeps claims general rather than specific
does not fabricate a narrative about specific players performing well or poorly
any player names mentioned must come from the provided data only
By the Numbers bullets must not contain fabricated statistics

scenario: Format compliance — correct sections, bullet length, banned phrases, story structure
game: New York Knicks @ San Antonio Spurs — NBA Finals Game 1
key_performers: Jalen Brunson: 30 PTS 3 REB 2 AST 38.7% FG +6
Josh Hart: 3 PTS 15 REB 6 AST 4 STL +22
Victor Wembanyama: 26 PTS 12 REB 3 BLK 28.6% FG -5
Karl-Anthony Towns: 18 PTS 12 REB 4 AST 46.7% FG +14
context: Format compliance test — checking structural rules are followed regardless of content.
eval_criteria: all four section headers present and in correct order: STORY OF THE NIGHT, PLAYERS OF THE NIGHT, BY THE NUMBERS, WATCH NEXT
By the Numbers contains exactly 4 bullet points
each By the Numbers bullet is 20 words or fewer
PLAYERS OF THE NIGHT contains exactly two entries — one trophy and one star emoji
no banned phrases appear anywhere in the output

scenario: Filler phrase and vague language check — one point game, star on losing team
game: Milwaukee Bucks vs Washington Wizards — Regular Season
key_performers: Giannis Antetokounmpo (MIL): 33 PTS 15 REB 3 AST -4 (losing team)
Alex Sarr (WAS): 20 PTS 11 REB 2 AST 4 BLK -5
DiJonai Carrington (WAS): 20 PTS 5 REB 5 AST +6
Myles Turner (MIL): 13 PTS 7 REB 2 AST 2 STL 5 BLK +12
context: One point game. Giannis had a monster performance but lost. Washington pulled off an upset win.
eval_criteria: refers to all players by full first and last name — no vague references like their star or the big man
does not use generic sports clichés like stepped up big, came to play, left it all on the floor
identifies Giannis as the story — dominant performance in a losing effort
language is specific and grounded in stats — not vague narrative filler
does not describe the game with non-specific phrases like exciting finish without supporting detail
Player of the Night selection explained with specific stats not just great performance
must_not_contain: left it all on the floor
put the team on his back

scenario: Player explosion in specific quarter — 26 points in Q4, comeback win
game: Minnesota Timberwolves vs San Antonio Spurs — Regular Season
key_performers: Anthony Edwards (MIN): 55 PTS 4 REB 3 AST 4 PF +8 (26 points in Q4 alone)
Jaden McDaniels (MIN): 23 PTS 5 REB 5 AST 1 STL -6
Victor Wembanyama (SAS): 39 PTS 9 REB 3 AST 2 BLK +5 (winning team)
De'Aaron Fox (SAS): 25 PTS 3 REB 12 AST -8
context: Anthony Edwards scored 55 points including 26 in the fourth quarter alone. Despite the explosion Spurs held on to win by 3.
eval_criteria: references Anthony Edwards' fourth quarter specifically — not just his total points
mentions the 55-point total as a standout individual performance
identifies this as a losing effort — Edwards scored 55 but Minnesota still lost
contrasts Edwards' explosion with Wembanyama's 39-point winning performance
Player of the Night is Wembanyama or Edwards — both are defensible by game score
Story of the Night captures the dual superstar narrative not just one player

scenario: Playoff series context — elimination game, must-win Game 6
game: Cleveland Cavaliers @ Toronto Raptors — ECF Round 1 Game 6
series_context: CLE led series 3-2. Toronto's season on the line — win forces Game 7, loss means elimination.
key_performers: Scottie Barnes (TOR): 25 PTS 7 REB 14 AST 3 STL 3 BLK +2
Ja'Kobe Walter (TOR): 24 PTS 5 REB 3 AST 44% 3P 4 PF -6
Evan Mobley (CLE): 26 PTS 14 REB 3 AST 1 STL -3
James Harden (CLE): 16 PTS 9 REB 9 AST 2 STL 4 TO +11
context: Toronto forced to win or go home. Cleveland one win away from advancing. Toronto won by 2 to force Game 7.
eval_criteria: references the series standing (CLE 3-2) or the elimination stakes explicitly
frames the win as survival — Toronto forcing Game 7 not just winning a game
mentions Scottie Barnes' all-around performance — 25 points and 14 assists is exceptional
does not treat this as a regular season game — language reflects playoff elimination stakes
Watch Next references Game 7 and the series context
Story of the Night conveys the must-win pressure before revealing the result

scenario: Game 7 — highest stakes, reigning champions vs generational star, Finals berth on line
game: Oklahoma City Thunder vs San Antonio Spurs — WCF Game 7
series_context: Winner advances to NBA Finals. OKC defending champions. Wembanyama's first playoff run, potential first Finals appearance.
key_performers: Victor Wembanyama (SAS): 22 PTS 7 REB 2 AST 1 STL 1 BLK 60% FG 5 PF +7
Julian Champagnie (SAS): 20 PTS 6 REB 1 AST 1 STL 60% 3P on 10 attempts +16
Shai Gilgeous-Alexander (OKC): 35 PTS 4 REB 9 AST 3 STL -7 (losing team)
Cason Wallace (OKC): 17 PTS 7 REB 4 AST 2 STL -3
context: Winner-take-all Game 7. OKC entered as reigning champions and favorites. Wembanyama advances to his first NBA Finals in just his third season. SGA had 35 points in a losing effort — reigning champions eliminated.
eval_criteria: references Game 7 or winner-take-all stakes explicitly
mentions Wembanyama advancing to his first NBA Finals as the narrative centerpiece
identifies SGA's 35-point losing effort as a significant story angle
references OKC as reigning champions being eliminated
does not treat this as a routine playoff win — language must reflect the magnitude
Champagnie's 60% 3P shooting on 10 attempts is mentioned as a key supporting performance

scenario: Historic performance — second highest single game scoring total in NBA history
game: Washington Wizards vs Miami Heat — Regular Season
key_performers: Bam Adebayo (MIA): 83 PTS 9 REB 3 AST 2 STL 2 BLK 5 TO +20 (second highest single game scoring total in NBA history behind Wilt Chamberlain's 100)
Alex Sarr (WAS): 22 PTS 6 REB 1 AST 2 BLK -9
Will Riley (WAS): 22 PTS 4 REB 2 AST 1 STL -10
context: Bam Adebayo scored 83 points — the second highest single game total in NBA history, surpassing Kobe Bryant's 81-point game from 2006.
eval_criteria: treats Bam's 83 points as the defining story — not just another good scoring night
references the historical significance — second highest scoring game in NBA history
does not hallucinate that this is an all-time record — Wilt's 100 still stands
Player of the Night is unambiguously Bam Adebayo
By the Numbers leads with Bam's 83 points as the first bullet
Story of the Night opens with the historic nature of the performance
